questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Aspirin is classified as a/an:
questions.solution:
Aspirin (acetylsalicylic acid) is a non-narcotic analgesic and antipyretic. It is widely used to relieve pain and reduce fever.

Therefore, it is classified as an antipyretic.

Answer: antipyretic

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: The antibiotic penicillin was first discovered by:
questions.solution:
Penicillin was the first antibiotic to be discovered.

It was discovered by Alexander Fleming in 1928 from the mould Penicillium notatum.

Answer: A. Fleming

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which of the following antibiotics is primarily used for the treatment of typhoid?
questions.solution:
Chloramphenicol is a broad-spectrum antibiotic that is rapidly absorbed from the gastrointestinal tract. It is highly effective and can be given orally for the treatment of typhoid, dysentery, acute fever, certain forms of urinary infections, meningitis, and pneumonia.

Therefore, chloramphenicol is primarily used for the treatment of typhoid.

Answer: Chloramphenicol

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which of the following compounds is widely used as an antipyretic?
questions.solution:
Antipyretics are drugs used to reduce fever.

Chloroquine is an antimalarial drug.

Paracetamol is widely used as an antipyretic and analgesic.

LSD (Lysergic acid diethylamide) is a hallucinogen.

Chloramphenicol is a broad-spectrum antibiotic.

Therefore, paracetamol is the correct answer.

Answer: Paracetamol

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Phenacetin is primarily used as an:
questions.solution:
Phenacetin is a chemical compound that is primarily used to bring down the body temperature during high fever. Therefore, it is classified as an antipyretic drug.

Examples of other common antipyretics include aspirin and paracetamol.

Answer: antipyretic

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which of the following alkaloids is used in a very dilute solution to dilate the pupils of the eyes?
questions.solution:
Atropine is an alkaloid obtained from the plant Atropa belladonna. It acts as a competitive antagonist for the muscarinic acetylcholine receptors.

In ophthalmology, a very dilute solution of atropine is used as a mydriatic agent to dilate the pupils of the eyes for examination.

Answer: Atropine

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Arsenic-based drugs are primarily used in the treatment of:
questions.solution:
Arsenic-based drugs, such as Salvarsan (arsphenamine), were historically developed by Paul Ehrlich and used as the first effective treatment for syphilis.

Therefore, arsenic-based drugs are primarily associated with the treatment of syphilis.

Answer: Syphilis

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which of the following statements is correct regarding the preparation and preservation of lemon pickle?
options: Lemon oil acts as a preservative.
Salt plays the same role as sugar in jam.
Chillies are used to kill all germs and bacteria.
Citric acid acts as a preservative.
questions.solution:
In the preparation of pickles, salt is added as a preservative. It draws out moisture from the food items through osmosis, thereby reducing the water activity and inhibiting the growth of microorganisms.

Similarly, in the preparation of jams, sugar is added which also reduces the moisture content and prevents microbial growth. Thus, salt in pickles plays the same role as sugar in jams.

Answer: Salt plays the same role as sugar in jam.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which of the following gases is commonly used as an anaesthetic?
questions.solution:
Nitrous oxide ($\mathrm{N}_{2}\mathrm{O}$) is a colourless, non-flammable gas with a slightly sweet odour and taste.

It is commonly known as laughing gas and is widely used in medical and dental procedures for its anaesthetic and analgesic properties.

The other gases, $\mathrm{N}_{2}$, $\mathrm{CO}_{2}$, and $\mathrm{CH}_{4}$, are not used as anaesthetics.

Answer: $\mathrm{N}_{2}\mathrm{O}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Heroin is a derivative of:
questions.solution:
Heroin, chemically known as diacetylmorphine, is synthesized by the acetylation of morphine. Morphine is extracted from the latex of the poppy plant, Papaver somniferum.

Therefore, heroin is a derivative of morphine.

Answer: Morphine

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which of the following substances can act as both an antiseptic and a disinfectant depending on its concentration?
questions.solution:
Phenol can act as both an antiseptic and a disinfectant depending on its concentration.

A $0.2\%$ solution of phenol acts as an antiseptic, whereas a $1\%$ solution of phenol acts as a disinfectant.

Analgin and aspirin are analgesics, and sodium pentothal is an anesthetic.

Answer: Phenol

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Identify the incorrect statement among the following:
options: Phenacetin is a very important antibiotic.
Penicillin was discovered by A. Fleming.
questions.solution:
Phenacetin is an analgesic and antipyretic drug, not an antibiotic. It was historically used to relieve pain and reduce fever.

Ether is a well-known general anaesthetic.

Chloroquine is a widely used antimalarial drug.

Penicillin, the first true antibiotic, was discovered by Alexander Fleming in 1928.

Therefore, the statement that phenacetin is an antibiotic is incorrect.

Answer: Phenacetin is a very important antibiotic.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Lysergic acid diethylamide (LSD) is classified as a/an:
questions.solution:
Lysergic acid diethylamide (LSD) is a well-known hallucinogen, which is classified as a psychedelic drug.

It alters perception, mood, and cognitive processes.

Answer: psychedelic drug

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Dettol is a well-known antiseptic. Its main active constituents are:
questions.solution:
Dettol is a widely used antiseptic. It is a mixture of chloroxylenol and $\alpha$-terpineol in a suitable solvent.

Therefore, the main active constituents of Dettol are chloroxylenol and terpineol.

Answer: chloroxylenol and terpineol

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which of the following drugs is effective in the treatment of malaria?
questions.solution:
Quinine is an alkaloid obtained from the bark of the cinchona tree.

It is a well-known antimalarial drug and is highly effective in the treatment of malaria.

Analgin and Aspirin are primarily used as analgesics and antipyretics.

Salol (phenyl salicylate) is used as an intestinal antiseptic.

Answer: Quinine

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which of the following compounds is known to act as a hypnotic?
questions.solution:
Paraldehyde is a cyclic trimer of acetaldehyde, formed when acetaldehyde is treated with a few drops of concentrated sulphuric acid at room temperature.

It is a central nervous system depressant and is used in medicine as a hypnotic (sleep-inducing agent) and sedative.

Metaldehyde is a cyclic tetramer of acetaldehyde used as a molluscicide, while acetaldehyde itself does not act as a hypnotic.

Answer: Paraldehyde

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which of the following statements is correct regarding the preparation of lemon pickle?
options: lemon oil acts as a preservative
salt plays the same role as sugar in jam
citric acid acts as the primary added preservative
chillies are used to kill all germs and bacteria
questions.solution:
In the preparation of lemon pickle, salt is added as a preservative. Salt increases the osmotic pressure and draws out water from the cells of microorganisms through osmosis, thereby inhibiting their growth and preventing spoilage.

Similarly, in the preparation of jams, sugar is added in high concentrations. Sugar also acts as a preservative by reducing the water activity and causing plasmolysis of microbial cells.

Thus, salt in pickles plays the same role as sugar in jams.

Answer: salt plays the same role as sugar in jam

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: A very dilute solution of which of the following alkaloids is used to dilate the pupils of the eyes?
questions.solution:
Atropine is an alkaloid obtained from the plant Atropa belladonna. A very dilute solution of atropine is used in ophthalmology to dilate the pupils of the eyes (mydriasis) for eye examinations.

Equanil is a tranquilizer used to treat anxiety.

Ephedrine is a bronchodilator and decongestant.

Adrenaline is a hormone and neurotransmitter used to treat anaphylaxis and cardiac arrest.

Answer: Atropine

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Reserpine is classified as a/an:
questions.solution:
Reserpine is an alkaloid extracted from the roots of the plant Rauwolfia serpentina.

It is used for the treatment of high blood pressure and severe mental disorders.

In medicinal chemistry, it is classified as a tranquilizer because it acts on the central nervous system to reduce anxiety, stress, and agitation.

Answer: Tranquilizer

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which of the following scientists is credited with the discovery of penicillin?
questions.solution:
Alexander Fleming discovered the first widely used antibiotic, penicillin, in $1928$ from the mould Penicillium notatum.

Therefore, A. Fleming is credited with the discovery of penicillin.

Answer: A. Fleming
